title: "When incoming email received from this column change status to something", only works for new emails, and not replies to an email I sent
excerpt:
When incoming email received from this column change status to something only works for new emails, and not replies to an email I sent. 
I want a status to change, when a customer replies to an email I sent, but it doesn…

posts.author: Ric (Ric)
posts.cookedHtml:
When incoming email received from this column change status to something only works for new emails, and not replies to an email I sent.

I want a status to change, when a customer replies to an email I sent, but it doesn’t work. However, the status does change if the customer sends me a “new” email.

Wondering if I’m missing something, or if anyone knows a work-around.

Thanks!

posts.author: Michael-PT (Michael Albers)
posts.cookedHtml: I think I am experiencing the same with setting a date column when I receive an email. I would like to see when I received the last email from a client and based on that date I want a notification if not already acted upon the last message.

posts.author: dmonlux (Dan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I had the same issues. I solved it by:

  1. creating a new column call “email flag” with status value of “New Mail” and “No Mail”
  2. created an automation that update email flag to new mail when an email reply is received.
  3. created an integration that updates a date column and then changes email flag back to No Mail, when the status changes to New Mail

posts.author: nomadicenergy (Charlie Low)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Dan, please can you explain how you set this up? You say “created an automation that update email flag to new mail when an email reply is received” - but I can’t see an option to create an automation based on a reply being received?

posts.author: mitchmann (Mitch Sayers)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to have a solution to this! I have a serious need for exactly this. Is there a different workaround? The solution outlined by Dan above does not appear to be an option any more.

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have set this up without any problems. If you have access to the automation/integration that is called “When email reply is received in this email column, set status to something”, just set it up to change a status column to “Reply received” or something.

Skjermbilde 2023-12-07 kl. 11.28.491068×556 27 KB

posts.author: charlie-at-Hue-hd (Charlie Van Norman)
posts.cookedHtml: Sjurd 's solution works for my case. Monday.com differentiates between “Email Received” and “Email Reply Received”. Make sure to set up your automation to trigger on “Email Reply Received” if you want this
